research.product: New Relic NerdGraph NRQL API (asynchronous queries)
research.interface: NerdGraph actor.account.nrql with async: true, nrqlQueryProgress polling and nrqlCancelQuery - current GA NerdGraph surface (official newrelic-client-go nrdb package as wire reference)

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tutorial: Run NRQL queries asynchronously | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the async: true argument on actor.account.nrql; the queryProgress selection {queryId, completed, retryAfter, retryDeadline, resultExpiration}; the default 5-second timeout with queryProgress returned when a query runs longer; polling via account.nrqlQueryProgress(queryId:) where the account must match the original query and the poll can be repeated verbatim until results or an error return; the nrqlCancelQuery mutation returning queryId/requestStatus/rejectionReason with ACCEPTED or REJECTED; the 10-minute maximum async duration

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tutorial: Query your data using NRQL | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the actor.account(id:).nrql(query:) shape, the mandatory results selection, the string NRQL argument, and the timeout argument on nrql

official_sources.title: Meet NerdGraph: our GraphQL API | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the regional endpoints https://api.newrelic.com/graphql and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql and the API-Key request header carrying a user API key

official_sources.title: newrelic-client-go pkg/nrdb/nrdb_api.go (official New Relic client sources)
official_sources.used_for: the exact GraphQL variable types the official client declares for NRQL queries: $accountId: Int!, $query: Nrql!, $timeout: Seconds, $async: Boolean

official_sources.title: newrelic-client-go pkg/nrdb/types.go (official New Relic client sources)
official_sources.used_for: the NRQL response container shapes: metadata {eventTypes, facets, messages, timeWindow {begin, compareWith, end, since, until}} and queryProgress {queryId, completed, retryAfter, retryDeadline, resultExpiration}

research.language_rationale: The reporting job runs in our Python 3 data-platform tooling. New Relic publishes no official Python SDK for NerdGraph and documents the API as plain GraphQL over HTTPS (its own curl examples), so a stdlib urllib client that mirrors the documented operations - with the official Go client's query text as wire reference - is the credible integration surface.

verified_facts: NRQL queries run through NerdGraph as actor.account(id:).nrql(query:) and the results field must be selected; the API is served at https://api.newrelic.com/graphql (US) and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql (EU) with a user API key in the API-Key header.

Passing async: true opts a query into the asynchronous workflow: if it finishes within the timeout (default 5 seconds) results return immediately, otherwise the response carries queryProgress {queryId, completed, retryAfter, retryDeadline, resultExpiration}.
Progress is polled with actor.account.nrqlQueryProgress(queryId:) on the same account as the original query, and the poll request can be repeated verbatim until results or an error are returned.
Async queries support a maximum duration of 10 minutes (Data Plus).
A running async query is cancelled with the nrqlCancelQuery(queryId:) mutation, which reports queryId, requestStatus (ACCEPTED or REJECTED) and rejectionReason.

The official New Relic Go client declares the NRQL query variables as $accountId: Int!, $query: Nrql!, $timeout: Seconds and $async: Boolean, and models response metadata as eventTypes, facets, messages and timeWindow {begin, compareWith, end, since, until}.

NerdGraph has no server-side NRQL parameter binding: the NRQL string is assembled client-side, so parameter values must be rendered through typed, quote-safe substitution and hostile strings rejected before any request is sent.
